verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer immediately: "When a network failure splits your database nodes apart... choose: keep answering with possibly stale data... or refuse to answer"; A is also strong but starts more conceptually with "CAP only forces a choice during a network partition."
verdict.decisiveness: B gives a direct decision rule: "Ask one question: If two parts of my system can't talk... wrong/stale answer, or no answer?" A also commits via "decide CP vs. AP per category of data," but B is faster to apply.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because it has a compact decision table: "Money, inventory, bookings... Choose CP" and "Likes, view counts, feeds... Choose AP," followed by a short bottom-line checklist.
